intitule: Identifying News Sources and Key Information

enonce.texte:
Read the short news item below and answer the questions.

📰 LOCAL NEWS

A big fire broke out in the Mvog-Mbi market in Yaoundé yesterday evening. The fire started at about 7 p.m. and destroyed many shops. Firefighters arrived quickly and stopped the fire. No one was hurt. The cause of the fire is not yet known.

questions.reponse: The fire happened in the Mvog-Mbi market in Yaoundé.
The fire started yesterday evening at about 7 p.m.

questions.texte: Who arrived to stop the fire?
questions.reponse: The firefighters arrived to stop the fire.

intitule: Asking and Answering Questions About News

enonce.texte: Complete the questions with the correct question word: Who, What, Where, When, Why, How.

Read the news article below and answer the questions.

📰 COMMUNITY NEWS

Last Saturday, the students of Government High School Molyko organised a clean-up campaign in their neighbourhood. They collected trash and cleaned the streets. The mayor of the town thanked them for their good work. The students said they wanted to keep their environment clean and healthy. The campaign started at 8 a.m. and ended at 12 p.m.

questions.reponse: The students of Government High School Molyko organised the clean-up campaign.

questions.texte: When did the campaign take place?
questions.reponse: The campaign took place last Saturday.

questions.texte: What did the students do during the campaign?
questions.reponse: They collected trash and cleaned the streets.
The mayor of the town thanked them.

enonce.texte: Write a short news report (4–5 sentences) about an event in your community. Include: what happened, where it happened, when it happened, and who was involved.

questions.texte: Write a short news report about a community event.
questions.reponse: Example: A football match was held at the town stadium last Sunday. Students from two schools played against each other. Many people came to watch the match. Our school team won 2-1. The principal congratulated the players.

sousQuestions.texte: Write 4–5 sentences including what, where, when, and who.

intitule: Asking and Answering Questions About a News Event

enonce.texte: Write a short dialogue between two friends discussing a news event. One person asks questions and the other gives answers.

questions.texte: Write a dialogue of about 6 exchanges.
questions.reponse: Example: A: Did you hear about the clean-up campaign? B: Yes, I did. It was organised by our school. A: Where did it take place? B: It took place in our neighbourhood. A: When did it happen? B: It happened last Saturday morning. A: Who participated? B: Many students and teachers participated. A: That's great! It helped keep our environment clean.

sousQuestions.texte: Write a dialogue with at least 6 exchanges.

intitule: Creating a News Flash for School Radio

enonce.texte: Write a short news flash script for your school radio. Include a headline, the main news, key details (who, what, where, when), and a closing line.

questions.texte: Write a news flash script (5–6 sentences).
questions.reponse: Example: Headline: GOOD NEWS FOR OUR SCHOOL! News: Our school football team won the regional championship yesterday. They played against Government High School and won 3-1. The match took place at the town stadium. Hundreds of students came to support the team. The principal said he is very proud of the players.

sousQuestions.texte: Write 5–6 sentences with a headline and key details.
